x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces OER's environmental-site / (E)-designation / cleanup-project data (BBL-keyed) and the remediation status of a cleanup, and adds request_notice_to_proceed — the net-new B2G write workflow (request a Notice to Proceed or enroll a brownfield site in the Voluntary Cleanup Program) that today runs manually through the login-walled EPIC portal. There is no general citizen write in this domain.

server.name: io.nyc.moer
server.title: NYC Mayor's Office of Environmental Remediation (MOER/OER)
server.description: Agent-native access to the NYC Mayor's Office of Environmental Remediation — the City's brownfield cleanup office. Look up environmental sites (the daily 'OER Cleanup Sites' feed and the SPEED map), check whether a property carries an (E)-designation (hazmat/noise/air) and whether it's resolved, read Voluntary Cleanup Program and E-Designation Program projects, and get where a cleanup stands in the remedial process. Adds the net-new ability to request a Notice to Proceed — or enroll a brownfield site in the VCP — that today runs manually through the login-walled EPIC portal.
server.instructions: Use find_environmental_sites / get_environmental_site to locate OER-tracked parcels by borough, program, phase, BBL, or free text (keyed on OER Project Number and BBL — OER is parcel-centric). find_e_designations / get_e_designation answers 'Do I have an E?' for a BBL — NOTE the authoritative (E) inventory is published under the DCP label, OER administers resolving it. find_cleanup_projects / get_cleanup_project cover VCP and E-Designation Program remedial projects. get_remediation_status returns the single 'where does this cleanup stand?' answer — current phase plus the determinations OER has issued (Notice of No Objection, Notice to Proceed, Notice of Satisfaction) and building-permit / C-of-O eligibility. find_notices lists issued OER determinations. request_notice_to_proceed (net-new) is a property-owner/B2G write — it requires an authenticated applicant and mutates state; confirm the property (BBL preferred), the request type (VCP enrollment vs. E-Designation/CEQR Notice to Proceed), and any (E)-designation number with the user before submitting. This agency serves owners, developers, and consultants preparing contaminated land for redevelopment: there is no general citizen service or write here.

tools.name: find_environmental_sites
tools.title: Find Environmental Sites
tools.description: Search OER-tracked environmental sites (the daily OER Cleanup Sites feed and the SPEED map) by borough, program, phase, BBL, and/or free text.

tools.name: get_environmental_site
tools.title: Get Environmental Site
tools.description: Fetch a single OER environmental site (program, phase, address, BBL/BIN, document repository) by OER Project Number.

tools.name: find_e_designations
tools.title: Find E-Designations
tools.description: Answer 'Do I have an E?' — look up (E)-designations and Restrictive Declarations (hazmat/noise/air) on a property by BBL. NOTE: the authoritative inventory is published under the DCP label; OER administers resolution.

tools.name: get_e_designation
tools.title: Get E-Designation
tools.description: Fetch a single (E)-designation / Restrictive Declaration by its (E) number.

tools.name: find_cleanup_projects
tools.title: Find Cleanup Projects
tools.description: Search OER cleanup projects (Voluntary Cleanup Program and E-Designation Program) by program, phase, borough, BBL, or free text.

tools.name: get_cleanup_project
tools.title: Get Cleanup Project
tools.description: Fetch a single OER cleanup project (program, phase, volunteer/consultant, (E)-designation cleared) by OER Project Number.

tools.name: get_remediation_status
tools.title: Get Remediation Status
tools.description: Return where a cleanup stands in the OER remedial process — current phase, the determinations issued (Notice of No Objection, Notice to Proceed, Notice of Satisfaction), and building-permit / certificate-of-occupancy eligibility. Today only reconstructable by reading EPIC portal documents.

tools.name: find_notices
tools.title: Find Notices
tools.description: List OER determinations issued on sites — Notice of No Objection, Notice to Proceed / Decision Document, Notice of Satisfaction — by type, OER Project Number, or BBL.

tools.name: list_my_notice_to_proceed_requests
tools.title: List My Notice to Proceed Requests
tools.description: List the authenticated applicant's own Notice to Proceed / VCP enrollment requests and their status — today visible only inside the EPIC portal.

tools.name: request_notice_to_proceed
tools.title: Request Notice to Proceed
tools.description: Request OER approval to proceed on a site — enroll a brownfield parcel in the Voluntary Cleanup Program, or request the Notice to Proceed / Decision Document (or Notice of No Objection) that clears an (E)-designation or CEQR requirement so a building permit can be issued. NET-NEW workflow with no OER API today — it runs manually through the login-walled EPIC portal and email. This is a property-owner/B2G transaction (no general citizen write in this domain). Requires an authenticated applicant. Mutates state — confirm the property (BBL preferred), request type, and any (E)-designation number first.

x-agent-skills.note: Each tool maps to one of the ten NYC common government-process Agent Skills via x-agent-skill.

prompts.name: explore_moer
prompts.description: Search and read NYC Environmental Remediation (OER) records — Sites, E-Designations, Cleanup Projects, Notices.

prompts.name: apply_for_moer
prompts.description: NoticeToProceedRequest — an owner/developer requests a Notice to Proceed (or enrolls a brownfield site in the Voluntary Cleanup Program) to proceed on a contaminated or (E)/CEQR-designated parcel. A B2G write, today a ma

prompts.name: check_moer_status
prompts.description: Check the status of a NYC Environmental Remediation (OER) application, case, or request.

resources.name: NYC Environmental Remediation (OER) — profile
resources.description: NYC Environmental Remediation (OER): 11 operations, 10 tools, verb “expose”.

resources.name: Sites record (templated)
resources.description: A single NYC Environmental Remediation (OER) Sites record by id.
